Olist E-commerce EDA

Sales Structure Analysis

Group 2

2026-06-23

  • Objective
  • 1. Sales Volume and Order Status
  • 2. Review Sentiment
  • 3. Geographic Concentration
  • 4. Macro Segments and Revenue
  • 5. Freight Burden and Weak-Category Diagnostics
  • 6. Delivery, Seller and Seasonality Diagnostics
  • 7. Transaction Clustering
  • Business Takeaways

Objective

The objective is to study the top performers and the products Olist has been struggling to sell.

1. Sales Volume and Order Status

Interpretation: High volume identifies commercial demand. Failure rate shows where demand may be leaking because orders are canceled or unavailable.

2. Review Sentiment

Interpretation: Sentiment adds customer experience context to volume. A category can sell well but still create dissatisfaction. From the plot we see that Furniture categories (office and decor) have the highest percentage of negative reviews which would require further analysis.

3. Geographic Concentration

Interpretation: Geographic concentration helps Sales decide where to focus seller coverage, stock placement and logistics improvement. From the plots, we see San Paolo has the most orders followed by MG, and RJ.

4. Macro Segments and Revenue

5. Freight Burden and Weak-Category Diagnostics

6. Delivery, Seller and Seasonality Diagnostics

7. Transaction Clustering

Business Takeaways

  • Weak categories need diagnosis: low volume may come from demand, but also from freight friction, reviews, delivery delays or seller issues.
  • Freight burden is a sales lever: categories where shipping is high relative to product price may benefit from bundling, minimum-order thresholds or logistics renegotiation.
  • Customer experience matters: review sentiment and delivery variance should be evaluated alongside sales volume before making assortment decisions.
